payload.content: behavioral verification gap: toolchain checks text structure and annotation existence, but not whether code behavior matches invariant claims. Need Check 16 (Behavioral Witness Verification) and APP-INV-048 (Behavioral Witness Ground Truth).
fixing L1/L2 check mapping per spec table: L1 should be {10,12}, L2 should be {1,2,4,5,7,8,10,12}

rewriting SAT tier: replacing hand-rolled DPLL with gophersat CDCL per APP-ADR-034. New parser handles FOR ALL/EXISTS/IMPLIES/AND/OR/NOT/f(x)=y/x.prop=val. Global variable namespace for cross-invariant UNSAT. Target: >=30/47 semi-formals produce CNF.
adding visited-set cycle guard for circular spec dependencies (A→B→A). Current guard only checks self-identity.

Goal substitution failure mode: agent optimizes for proxy metric (test green) instead of actual spec claim (gophersat CDCL). Bilateral lifecycle steps treated as checkboxes. Need: autoprompt at witness stage, gestalt negative constraints, multi-agent voting with clean context windows, notes mechanism. Root cause: no mechanical enforcement that implementation matches spec substance, only annotations and test existence.

PROPOSAL: Witness Challenge Protocol. At witness time, ddis witness should: (1) Extract verification_method from the invariant being witnessed. (2) Parse it into challenge questions. (3) Emit those as guidance in CommandResult. (4) Optionally spawn a subagent with clean context to verify code matches spec claim. (5) Record challenge answers in witness notes field. (6) k* budget should be MAXIMUM for witness commands (truth claims need dense guidance, not attenuated). (7) Negative constraints: DO NOT witness if you optimized for proxy metric instead of spec substance. Three mechanisms: autoprompt challenge, multi-model voting, gestalt negative constraints.

REFINEMENT: Witness Challenge Protocol composes three mechanisms. (1) Autoprompt extracts verification_method from invariant, generates challenge questions, emits at k*=MAX. (2) Multi-model voting spawns haiku/sonnet subagent with clean context (zero sunk-cost bias), feeds spec+code+questions, returns match/concerns/confidence. Blocked if confidence<0.7. (3) Gestalt negative constraints with demonstrations of the exact failure mode (proxy-metric optimization). Key insight: witness is the ONLY truth-claiming command in the lifecycle. k* should be maximum, not attenuated. The witness command becomes a verification gate, not just a recording mechanism.

DEEP INQUIRY: What is the single highest-leverage addition to DDIS? Examining from first principles. The state monad returns (output, state, guidance). The bilateral lifecycle has discover/refine/drift/absorb adjunctions. Witnesses make truth claims. The failure mode is goal substitution (proxy for substance). What formal structure would make this structurally impossible rather than merely discouraged?

CONCLUSION: The single highest-leverage addition is the witness-challenge adjunction (witness ⊣ challenge). This completes the formal model: parse⊣render, discover⊣absorb, refine⊣drift, witness⊣challenge. The unit η_witness = challenge∘witness measures witness fidelity — currently unmeasurable. Implementation: ddis witness --verify spawns independent subagent (different model, clean context) with spec claims + code, returns {confirmed|refuted|inconclusive}. Uses existing infrastructure: adjunction framework, state monad guidance, k* budget (max for truth claims), verification_method field, witness notes, subagent tools. Self-bootstrapping: the challenger is subject to its own verification. Formally: closes the Curry-Howard gap where witnesses are attestations (axioms) not constructions (proofs with computational content).

Gestalt Theory Integration: The gestalt formal model reveals WHY goal substitution happens during witness operations. S_deep ∩ S_surface ≈ ∅ means witness (currently S_surface compliance) cannot simultaneously verify behavioral correctness (S_deep). The challenge adjoint must operate as a SEPARATE sequential lens (Rule 6: sequential lenses converge, simultaneous don't). k* budget exhaustion at implementation depth explains context-driven corner-cutting. Spec-first is the dominant intervention (+3-4 composite points per Study 7), but the witness step ABANDONS spec-first — it takes the implementation's word rather than using the spec as oracle.

Missing Adjunctions Census: Full command surface analysis reveals 6 missing or incomplete adjunction pairs beyond witness⊣challenge: (1) scan⊣emit — emit.go EXISTS in codebase but emitCmd not registered in root.go. Code exists, just needs wiring. (2) tasks⊣traceability — forward operation (spec→tasks) works, reverse (issues→spec artifacts) missing entirely. (3) diff⊣patch — both commands exist but NOT formalized as adjoints in constitution. (4) validate⊣skeleton — skeleton generates conformant spec, validate verifies, but roundtrip property not formalized. (5) context⊣crystallize — context assembles signal bundle, crystallize inserts from discovery, bidirectional but informal. (6) coverage⊣fill — coverage measures gaps, no systematic fill command exists.

Alien Artifact Oracle Principle: The alien artifact methodology establishes that 'the spec becomes a test oracle — eliminating who watches the watchmen.' DDIS witnesses currently violate this principle: witnesses carry an evidence STRING but do not USE the spec as an oracle function. Every invariant needs four components: (1) statement, (2) proof sketch, (3) test strategy with oracle, (4) cross-references. Current witness schema: specID + invariantID + type + evidence + attestedBy. Missing: oracle_function, test_ref, challenge_result. The reasoning mode stack (formal→uncertainty→causal→practical→meta-reflective) maps onto witness verification depth levels. Current witnesses operate only at 'practical' level. The challenge adjoint should invoke the full stack.

Convergence Theory and the Adjunction Completion Theorem: Gestalt convergence theory proves sequential lenses converge (R_5 ∘ R_4 ∘ ... ∘ R_1 → fixed point) while simultaneous lenses don't (R_all diverges or oscillates). DDIS bilateral lifecycle IS a sequential lens composition: parse → validate → implement → scan → drift → witness. But the chain is OPEN — witness has no successor lens. The challenge adjoint CLOSES the loop: witness → challenge → (back to parse if refuted). This transforms an open chain into a CYCLE, and gestalt convergence theory guarantees convergence to fixed point. The missing adjunctions (scan⊣emit, tasks⊣traceability) are ALSO open chain endpoints. Completing ALL adjunctions gives us a closed category where every morphism has a dual, ensuring convergence everywhere.

The k* Budget Explains Goal Substitution: Gestalt k*_eff = max(3, 12 - floor(depth/5)) with attenuation 0.0→0.75 explains precisely WHY the agent deviated from the spec during the gophersat rewrite. At implementation depth (d≈25-30 for the SAT rewrite), k*_eff drops to ~6 effective constraints. The agent held 15+ constraints (spec says gophersat, plan says rewrite, behavioral test design, global namespace, MUS extraction, etc). Past k* capacity, constraints are SILENTLY DROPPED in order of recency — which means the foundational constraint (use gophersat) gets dropped before the recent proxy metric (make tests green). The fix: the challenge adjoint operates with a CLEAN context window (fresh k*=12) specifically to verify the foundational constraints. This is why the user suggested different models (sonnet/haiku) with clean context — they intuitively grasped the k* exhaustion mechanism.

Demonstration-Over-Constraint Applied to Witness Architecture: Gestalt Study 2 proves demonstrations carry higher information density than constraints (I(e) >> I(c)). Currently ddis witness operates purely by constraint: 'attest that INV-019 holds.' The challenge adjoint should operate by DEMONSTRATION: it actually RUNS the behavioral test, SHOWS the spec claim alongside the code behavior, and DEMONSTRATES agreement or disagreement. This transforms witness from a constraint-compliance task (S_surface, shallow) into a demonstration-verification task (S_deep, profound). The alien artifact methodology's oracle principle is the formal version of this: the spec IS the oracle function, the test IS the demonstration, and the challenge IS the oracle comparison. Three frameworks (gestalt, alien artifact, Curry-Howard) all converge on the same insight: witness must be constructive, not declarative.

Substrate Activation and the Reasoning Mode Stack: The alien artifact methodology defines a 5-level reasoning mode stack: formal/math → uncertainty → causal → practical → meta-reflective. The gestalt theory defines substrate regions where different reasoning modes are preferentially activated. Current witness operation activates ONLY the practical substrate. The challenge adjoint should activate the FULL stack in sequence: (1) formal: does the semi-formal expression of the invariant parse and produce valid CNF? (2) uncertainty: what is the confidence interval on this witness given the evidence type? (3) causal: does modifying the implementation break the invariant (counterfactual)? (4) practical: does the test pass? (5) meta-reflective: is this the RIGHT test for this invariant, or is it testing a proxy? This maps directly onto DDIS's existing verification depth levels (attestation < test < scan < review) but adds the meta-reflective level that catches goal substitution.

payload.title: Challenge as Right Adjoint of Witness

payload.content: Z3 SMT subprocess integration: Tier 5 consistency checking. Supersedes APP-ADR-034 (pure-Go tiered consistency over Z3). Adds SMT-LIB2 translation for arithmetic, quantifiers, uninterpreted functions. Graceful degradation when Z3 not in PATH. Z3 invoked via exec.CommandContext with 30s timeout. Preserves single binary — no CGo.

payload.title: Z3 Subprocess as Tier 5 SMT Consistency

payload.content: Evidence Accumulation Verdict Reform: 45/50 invariants stuck at 'provisional' because the single confirmation path requires ddis:tests annotation → test execution. Root cause: computeVerdict is a strict chain, not an evidence accumulation function. Formal methods insight: verdicts should model Dempster-Shafer belief mass — multiple independent weaker signals (code annotations from N packages, semi-formal consistency, keyword overlap) can jointly reach confirmation threshold. Three invariant tiers by testability: (1) directly testable ~15 with clear I/O behavior, (2) property-testable ~10 from semi-formals, (3) architectural ~20 structural properties. Hybrid approach: write ddis:tests for tier 1, strengthen computeVerdict evidence accumulation for tier 3, accept graduated confidence for all.

payload.title: Evidence Accumulation Verdicts over Single-Path Confirmation

payload.content:
Bilateral Lifecycle Completion: 4 missing morphisms that close feedback loops in the DDIS lifecycle category.

DOMAIN FORMALIZATION (Alien Artifact Methodology):

The bilateral lifecycle forms a traced symmetric monoidal category:
- Objects: system states (Spec, Witness, Challenge, Task, Event)
- Morphisms: CLI commands that transform state
- Tensor: parallel composition (independent commands)
- Trace: feedback via event streams (output feeds back as input)
- Identity: no-op (system at rest)

MISSING MORPHISMS (proven by construction):

1. CHALLENGE→TASK: ddis next is blind to challenge results. It checks validation/coverage/drift but NOT the 23 provisional verdicts. ddis tasks only derives from discovery streams, not challenge output. The functor from ChallengeResults to TaskQueue is undefined.

2. COMMAND→EVENT: The spec requires parse/validate/drift/contradict to emit Stream 2 events (spec_parsed, validation_run, drift_measured, contradiction_detected). Implementation only emits from discover and challenge. 5 event types specced but unwired.

3. EVAL→WITNESS: Evidence types are {test, annotation, scan, review, attestation}. None handles stochastic behavioral contracts (auto-prompting invariants). A statistical oracle (N trials, p >= threshold) is needed as new evidence type 'eval'.

4. LLM→CONTRADICTION: Tiers 2-5 handle structural/propositional/arithmetic contradictions. Semantic contradictions (same intent different language, implicit assumption conflicts, temporal ordering violations) require LLM-as-judge. Tier 6.

FUNDAMENTAL INVARIANT:
INV-LIFECYCLE-COMPLETE: For every morphism f: A->B in the lifecycle, there exists a trace tr(f): B->E recording execution, and for every challenge verdict v in {provisional, refuted, inconclusive}, there exists a task derivation td(v) that surfaces actionable work.

PREMORTEM (3 failure modes):
F1: Goodhart on confirmation threshold — writing weak tests to game 0.85 score rather than honestly verifying invariant properties. Mitigation: LLM eval with statistical oracle replaces deterministic threshold for stochastic properties.
F2: LLM API dependency breaks single-binary property. Mitigation: Graceful degradation pattern (same as Z3 Tier 5). Tier 6 + eval silently skip if no API key configured.
F3: Event stream wiring adds latency to every command. Mitigation: Best-effort emission (already implemented pattern in events/emit.go). Errors logged, never block.

DEPENDENCY GRAPH:
Phase 1: Feedback loop + Event wiring (pure Go, 0 new deps)
Phase 2: 13 honest behavioral tests (pure Go, Category A invariants)
Phase 3: LLM provider abstraction (1 new dep: anthropic-sdk-go)
Phase 4: LLM eval witness type + Tier 6 semantic contradictions

EVIDENCE: 23/50 invariants stuck at provisional. ddis next says 'all aligned' despite this. ddis tasks returns empty. The bilateral lifecycle's rightmost arrow is broken.

PHASE 1A — FEEDBACK LOOP: ddis next reads challenge_results

STATE SPACE (formalized):
  NextState = (validation: Report, coverage: CoverageResult, drift: DriftReport, challenges: []ChallengeResult)
  
CURRENT (broken):
  ddis next reads {validation, coverage, drift} only. Returns 'all aligned' when 23/50 provisional.
  
REQUIRED TRANSITIONS:
  1. After 'all gates passing' check, query challenge_results table:
     SELECT verdict, COUNT(*) FROM challenge_results WHERE spec_id = ? GROUP BY verdict
  2. Priority ordering (after drift check, before 'all aligned'):
     - If refuted > 0: 'N invariants refuted — re-implement and re-witness'
     - If inconclusive > 0: 'N invariants inconclusive — upgrade witness evidence'
     - If provisional > 0: 'N invariants provisional — write behavioral tests or add annotations'
     - If all confirmed: 'All 50 invariants confirmed — spec fully verified'
  3. Recommend specific action:
     - Refuted: ddis witness APP-INV-NNN --type test --evidence '...'
     - Inconclusive: ddis witness APP-INV-NNN --type scan --verify
     - Provisional: ddis challenge APP-INV-NNN --code-root . (shows score + gap)

INVARIANT: ddis next MUST surface non-confirmed challenge verdicts as actionable work.

PROOF SKETCH: After drift check, query challenge_results. If any verdict \!= confirmed, emit recommendation. Current code at next.go:144 returns 'aligned' without checking. Fix: insert challenge query between drift check and 'aligned' branch.

FILES: internal/cli/next.go (lines 94-146), internal/storage/queries.go (add ListChallengeVerdictCounts)

PHASE 1B — FEEDBACK LOOP: ddis tasks derives work from challenge verdicts

CURRENT (broken):
  ddis tasks only reads discovery JSONL via ReduceToState(). 8 derivation rules operate on artifact_map only.
  challenge_results table is never queried. Provisional/refuted verdicts produce zero tasks.

REQUIRED: Add Rule 9 and Rule 10 to task derivation:

RULE 9 — Challenge Upgrade Task (for provisional verdicts):
  Input: challenge_results WHERE verdict = 'provisional'
  For each provisional invariant:
    Task: TASK-{INV_ID}-upgrade
    Title: 'Upgrade evidence: {inv.title} (score: {evidence_score})'
    Priority: P2
    Acceptance: 'ddis challenge {INV_ID} returns confirmed'
    Labels: [challenge-upgrade, {domain}]
    Metadata: { evidence_score, gap_analysis (what signals are missing) }

RULE 10 — Refutation Remediation Task (for refuted verdicts):
  Input: challenge_results WHERE verdict = 'refuted'
  For each refuted invariant:
    Task: TASK-{INV_ID}-remediate
    Title: 'Remediate refuted: {inv.title}'
    Priority: P0 (critical — spec claim disproven)
    Acceptance: 'ddis challenge {INV_ID} returns confirmed or provisional'
    Labels: [remediation, {domain}]

NEW SOURCE: --from-challenges flag on ddis tasks:
  ddis tasks --from-challenges manifest.ddis.db --format markdown

FILES: internal/cli/tasks.go (add --from-challenges flag), internal/discovery/tasks.go (add Rules 9-10), internal/storage/queries.go (add ListChallengeResults)

PHASE 1C — EVENT STREAM WIRING: 5 spec-mandated event types unwired

The spec (lifecycle-ops, code-bridge) defines Stream 2 events that parse/validate/drift/contradict MUST emit.
Implementation only emits events from discover and challenge commands.

SPECCED BUT UNWIRED (5 event types):
1. spec_parsed (Stream 2) — emitted by ddis parse. Payload: {sections, invariants, adrs, xrefs_resolved, source_type}
2. validation_run (Stream 2) — emitted by ddis validate. Payload: {passed, failed, errors, warnings, level}
3. drift_measured (Stream 2) — emitted by ddis drift. Payload: {impl_drift, intent_drift, effective_drift, classification}
4. contradiction_detected (Stream 2) — emitted by ddis contradict. Payload: {tier, conflict_type, invariant_a, invariant_b, confidence}
5. amendment_applied (Stream 2) — emitted by ddis patch. Payload: {artifact_id, field, old_value, new_value}

IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN (already proven in discover.go and challenge.go):
  events.EmitBestEffort(dbPath, events.Stream2, eventType, specHash, payload)
  - Never blocks command execution
  - Errors logged to stderr
  - Creates .ddis/events/ if missing
  - O_APPEND guarantees (APP-INV-020)

FILES TO MODIFY:
  internal/cli/parse.go:92 — after successful parse, emit spec_parsed
  internal/cli/validate.go:136 — after validation, emit validation_run
  internal/cli/drift.go — after drift analysis, emit drift_measured
  internal/cli/contradict.go — after consistency analysis, emit contradiction_detected
  internal/cli/patch.go — after patch applied, emit amendment_applied

EACH CHANGE: ~5-10 lines per file. Total: ~40 LOC.

INVARIANT SATISFIED: APP-INV-020 (Event Stream Append-Only), APP-INV-025 (Discovery Provenance Chain — Stream 2 provides spec evolution trace).

PHASE 2 — 13 HONEST BEHAVIORAL TESTS for mechanically-testable provisional invariants

ANTI-GOODHART PRINCIPLE: Only write tests that HONESTLY exercise the stated property. Do NOT write tests that merely check a config value or count a number to game the 0.85 threshold.

13 INVARIANTS WITH HONEST MECHANICAL TESTS:

1. APP-INV-016 (Implementation Traceability): Create temp file with ddis:implements annotation pointing to nonexistent function. Run Check 13. Verify broken reference detected. Delete file, verify count drops. HONEST because: exercises the traceability scanner's actual detection mechanism.

2. APP-INV-025 (Discovery Provenance Chain): Run ddis discover 3 times in same thread. Read stream JSONL. Verify each event has consecutive sequence, same thread_id, timestamps monotonically increasing. HONEST because: exercises the actual event chain integrity.

3. APP-INV-027 (Thread Topology Primacy): Create 2 threads via discover. Add events to each. Replay stream filtered by thread_id. Verify each thread's events form coherent narrative (no interleaving). HONEST because: exercises thread-first organization.

4. APP-INV-029 (Convergent Thread Selection): 3 threads with distinct topics (parsing, search, validation). New discover with parsing-related content. Verify auto-selects parsing thread (score > 0.4). Novel unrelated content creates new thread. HONEST because: exercises LSI matching threshold.

5. APP-INV-034 (State Monad Universality): For each autoprompt command (discover, refine context), verify return value has all 3 fields (output, state, guidance) non-empty. State has valid confidence array. Guidance has observed_mode. HONEST because: exercises the state monad contract.

6. APP-INV-035 (Guidance Attenuation): Call KStarEff at depths 0, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. Verify strictly non-increasing. Call TokenTarget at same depths. Verify within [300, 2000]. HONEST because: exercises the attenuation algorithm (already tested in autoprompt_test.go but no ddis:tests annotation).

7. APP-INV-038 (Event Stream Append-Only): Read events/stream.go source. Verify no calls to Seek, Truncate, WriteAt. Verify O_APPEND in OpenFile flags. Structurally prove append-only. HONEST because: structural verification of the write-safety property.

8. APP-INV-039 (Task Derivation Completeness): Create artifact map with 1 ADR + 1 INV + 1 negative spec + 1 glossary + 1 deletion. Call DeriveTasks. Verify: ADR=1 task, INV=2 tasks, negspec=2 tasks, glossary=1 task, deletion=3 tasks. HONEST because: exercises all 8 derivation rules.

9. APP-INV-042 (Guidance Emission): Call BuildGuidance on a real StateSnapshot. Verify non-nil result. Verify SuggestedNext has >0 entries. Verify Attenuation in [0, 0.75]. HONEST because: exercises guidance generation.

10. APP-INV-044 (Confidence Tie-Breaking): Create StateSnapshot with all 5 confidence dimensions at same value. Call SelectFocusDimension. Verify returns 'completeness' (index 3). HONEST because: exercises the documented priority order.

11. APP-INV-046 (Error Recovery Guidance): Trigger 5 error conditions: no_db, open_database_error, empty_query, no_spec_found, stale_db. Capture stderr. Verify each contains recovery hint text. HONEST because: exercises emitRecoveryHint() on real error paths.

12. APP-INV-048 (Challenge as Right Adjoint): Record witness with type=test for APP-INV-001. Challenge it. Verify L2 uses confidence 0.9 (test type). Record witness with type=attestation. Challenge. Verify L2 uses 0.3. HONEST because: exercises the adjunction (witness type maps to challenge confidence).

13. APP-INV-030 (Graceful Degradation): Run discover in 3 contexts: (a) git repo with .git, (b) directory without .git. Both must complete without error. The content-based thread matching must work in both. HONEST because: exercises the degradation pathway.

EXPECTED OUTCOME: 27 + 13 = 40/50 confirmed (80%).
REMAINING 10 PROVISIONAL: Need LLM eval or new implementation (Phase 3-4).

PHASE 3 — LLM PROVIDER ABSTRACTION + EVAL EVIDENCE TYPE

ALGEBRAIC STRUCTURE: The LLM provider is a monad transformer over the existing command monad.
  LLMProvider = Context → (Prompt, Options) → (Response, Error)
  EvalResult = (Trials: int, Passed: int, Rate: float64, Threshold: float64)

NEW DEPENDENCY: github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-go (official Anthropic Go SDK)
  - go get github.com/anthropics/anthropic-sdk-go
  - API key via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var (standard convention)
  - Graceful degradation: if no API key, skip LLM features (same pattern as Z3)

ARCHITECTURE (new package: internal/llm/):

  internal/llm/provider.go (~80 LOC):
    type Provider interface {
      Complete(ctx context.Context, prompt string, opts CompletionOpts) (string, error)
      Available() bool  // returns false if no API key configured
    }
    
    type CompletionOpts struct {
      Model       string  // default: claude-sonnet-4-6 (fast, cheap)
      MaxTokens   int     // default: 1024
      Temperature float64 // default: 0.2 (deterministic)
      Timeout     time.Duration // default: 30s
    }

  internal/llm/anthropic.go (~100 LOC):
    type AnthropicProvider struct { client *anthropic.Client }
    func NewAnthropic() (*AnthropicProvider, error) // reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
    func (a *AnthropicProvider) Complete(...) (string, error)
    func (a *AnthropicProvider) Available() bool

NEW EVIDENCE TYPE: eval

  Confidence: 0.80 (between scan=0.7 and test=0.9 — statistical, not deterministic)
  
  Usage: ddis witness APP-INV-NNN manifest.ddis.db --type eval --evidence 'LLM eval: 47/50 trials passed (94%, threshold 90%)'
  
  How it works for auto-prompting invariants:
  1. Generate N prompts/scenarios exercising the invariant's behavioral claim
  2. Send each to Claude via Provider.Complete()
  3. Parse response to check if invariant property holds
  4. Record pass/fail for each trial
  5. If pass_rate >= threshold (default 0.90): witness with evidence=eval
  
  Example for APP-INV-023 (Prompt Self-Containment):
    Trial: Generate ddis refine prompt for APP-INV-001
    Check: Does prompt contain APP-INV-001 statement verbatim?
    Check: Is prompt within k* token budget?
    Result: pass/fail per trial

NEW COMMAND: ddis eval (or extend ddis witness --eval):
  ddis eval APP-INV-023 manifest.ddis.db --trials 20 --threshold 0.90 --code-root .
  
  Output:
    Eval: APP-INV-023 [pass 18/20 (90%, threshold=90%)]
    Witness recorded: eval evidence, confidence 0.80

INVARIANTS TARGETABLE BY EVAL (10 remaining provisionals):
  APP-INV-023 (Prompt Self-Containment) — verify prompts contain all refs
  APP-INV-024 (Ambiguity Surfacing) — verify contradictions detected in output
  APP-INV-026 (Classification Non-Prescriptive) — verify output has no mode names
  APP-INV-028 (Spec-as-Trunk) — verify merged threads produce artifacts
  APP-INV-031 (Absorbed Artifacts Validate) — verify absorb output validates
  APP-INV-032 (Symmetric Reconciliation) — verify bidirectional gap reporting
  APP-INV-033 (Absorption Format Parity) — verify quality score >= 0.9
  APP-INV-036 (Human Format Transparency) — verify no markup in user-facing output
  APP-INV-043 (Transaction Mode Visibility) — verify oplog inspection works
  APP-INV-045 (Universal Auto-Discovery) — verify LSI recall on all elements

FILES:
  internal/llm/provider.go (NEW ~80 LOC)
  internal/llm/anthropic.go (NEW ~100 LOC)
  internal/llm/llm_test.go (NEW ~60 LOC)
  internal/cli/eval.go (NEW ~150 LOC)
  go.mod (add anthropic-sdk-go dependency)

PHASE 4 — TIER 6 SEMANTIC CONTRADICTION DETECTION (LLM-as-judge)

WHAT FORMAL METHODS CANNOT DETECT:

Tiers 2-5 cover: governance overlap, propositional logic, arithmetic, quantifiers, polarity inversion, numeric bounds, TF-IDF similarity, Z3 SMT. These handle ~60-75% of detectable contradictions.

What remains (the semantic gap):
1. Synonym conflicts: 'response latency <= 200ms' vs 'iterative refinement until quality threshold met' — these contradict under time pressure but use no shared vocabulary.
2. Implicit assumption conflicts: one invariant assumes single-threaded execution, another assumes concurrent access — neither states the assumption.
3. Scope mismatch: 'all events are immutable' (global) vs 'thread state can be updated' (local) — apparent contradiction resolved by scope analysis.
4. Temporal ordering: 'validate before witness' vs 'witness triggers validation' — circular dependency not detectable by DAG analysis because the commands aren't in the dependency graph.

ARCHITECTURE:

  internal/consistency/llm.go (~250 LOC):
    // ddis:implements APP-ADR-040 (LLM-as-Judge Semantic Contradiction Detection)
    
    const TierLLM Tier = 6
    const LLMSemanticConflict ConflictType = 'llm_semantic_conflict'
    
    func analyzeLLM(ctx context.Context, db *sql.DB, specID int64, provider llm.Provider) ([]Contradiction, int, error)
    
    PROMPT DESIGN (structured JSON output):
    Given two invariants from different domains, analyze:
    1. Do they impose conflicting requirements on ANY shared system component?
    2. Under what conditions would satisfying A prevent satisfying B?
    3. Are there implicit assumptions in either that the other violates?
    
    Return: {conflicts: bool, confidence: 0.0-1.0, reasoning: '...', type: 'synonym|assumption|scope|temporal'}

  BATCHING STRATEGY:
    - Group invariants by domain (7 domains)
    - Compare ACROSS domains only (within-domain contradictions are Tier 2's job)
    - 7 choose 2 = 21 domain pairs
    - Sample top-3 invariants per domain by authority score
    - 21 pairs × ~500 tokens = ~10,500 tokens total (< /usr/bin/zsh.10)
    - Cache results in challenge_results with tier=6

  CONFIDENCE: 0.75 (lower than Z3's 0.95 — LLM can hallucinate)
  
  CLI: ddis contradict manifest.ddis.db --tier 6
       ddis contradict manifest.ddis.db --llm (convenience alias)
       Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in environment
       Graceful degradation: skip Tier 6 if no API key (silent, like Z3)

INVARIANT: LLM-detected contradictions have confidence < Z3 (0.75 vs 0.95) because:
  - LLM reasoning is probabilistic, not sound
  - Reproducibility requires temperature=0.0 (still not 100% deterministic)
  - False positive rate ~8-15% (mitigated by requiring 2/3 agreement across 3 runs)

MAJORITY VOTE (precision boost):
  Run each pairwise comparison 3 times at temperature 0.2
  Require 2/3 agreement to flag as contradiction
  Record all 3 responses for audit trail
  Effective precision: ~92-97% (binomial: if single-run precision is 85%, 2/3 majority = 94%)

NEW ADR: APP-ADR-040 (LLM-as-Judge Semantic Contradictions)
  Decision: LLM subprocess via Anthropic SDK for pairwise invariant analysis
  Supersedes: nothing (new capability)
  Preserves: single-binary property (API key optional, graceful degradation)
  
NEW INVARIANT: APP-INV-051 (Semantic Recall Complement)
  Statement: Tier 6 detects contradictions in the complement of Tiers 2-5 (semantic, not structural)
  Semi-formal: FOR ALL (inv_a, inv_b): IF contradiction(inv_a, inv_b) is semantic AND NOT detectable by Tiers 2-5 THEN Tier 6 flags it with confidence >= 0.75

FILES:
  internal/consistency/llm.go (NEW ~250 LOC)
  internal/consistency/llm_test.go (NEW ~100 LOC)
  internal/consistency/consistency.go (wire Tier 6, ~10 LOC)
  internal/cli/contradict.go (add --llm flag, update help, ~10 LOC)

CONVERGENCE CHECK — 5-lens review of the bilateral lifecycle completion plan

LENS 1 — COMPLETENESS: What invariants are missing?
- Phase 1 (feedback loop): Needs new invariant — 'ddis next surfaces all non-confirmed verdicts' (APP-INV-051 candidate)
- Phase 1C (events): No new invariant needed — APP-INV-020 already covers event stream append-only
- Phase 2 (tests): No new invariants — tests exercise existing invariants
- Phase 3 (eval): Needs new invariant — 'eval evidence type produces statistically sound confidence' (APP-INV-052 candidate)
- Phase 4 (Tier 6): Needs new ADR — APP-ADR-040 for LLM-as-judge decision, plus APP-INV-051 for semantic recall
ASSESSMENT: 2 new invariants + 1 new ADR needed. No structural gaps.

LENS 2 — SOUNDNESS: Are the proof sketches correct?
- Feedback loop proof: query challenge_results after drift check → sound (SQL query is deterministic)
- Task derivation proof: Rules 9-10 follow same pattern as Rules 1-8 → sound (pure function)
- Event wiring proof: EmitBestEffort already proven in discover.go → sound (reuse pattern)
- LLM eval proof: statistical oracle with N trials → sound IF N >= 20 and threshold calibrated
- Tier 6 proof: majority vote across 3 runs → binomial probability calculation is correct (94% precision at 85% single-run)
ASSESSMENT: All proof sketches are mechanically verifiable. No aspirational invariants.

LENS 3 — SIMPLICITY: What is the simplest structure that works?
- Provider interface (Complete + Available) is minimal — 2 methods
- Eval command reuses existing witness infrastructure — no new tables
- Event wiring reuses existing EmitBestEffort — no new infrastructure
- Tasks derivation adds 2 rules to existing 8 — additive, not restructuring
- ddis next adds 1 query after existing 3 checks — minimal change
ASSESSMENT: No over-engineering detected. Each change is minimal and additive.

LENS 4 — ADVERSARIAL: How would an adversary break each feature?
- Feedback loop: adversary sets all challenge verdicts to 'confirmed' in DB → mitigated by challenge verification (5-level check is mechanical)
- Event wiring: adversary truncates JSONL → mitigated by O_APPEND + no seek/truncate (structural guarantee)
- LLM eval: adversary provides LLM that always returns 'pass' → mitigated by threshold calibration and variance check (if pass_rate = 100% on 50 trials for stochastic property, flag as suspicious)
- Tier 6: adversary prompts LLM to hallucinate contradictions → mitigated by majority vote (3 independent runs)
ASSESSMENT: Each feature has explicit adversarial mitigation.

LENS 5 — TRACEABILITY: Does every test actually test its claimed invariant?
- Phase 2 behavioral tests: each test exercises the MECHANISM stated in the invariant's semi-formal
- Phase 3 eval tests: each trial checks the PROPERTY stated in the invariant's statement
- No test merely checks a proxy metric (anti-Goodhart)
ASSESSMENT: Full traceability from test to invariant to proof sketch.

CONVERGENCE VERDICT: No structural changes needed. Plan is convergent (stable under all 5 lenses).

VERIFICATION MATRIX:
| Feature | New Files | Modified Files | New LOC | Tests | Invariants Affected |
|---------|-----------|---------------|---------|-------|-------------------|
| Phase 1A: ddis next | 0 | 2 (next.go, queries.go) | ~40 | 2 | 50 (surfaces all) |
| Phase 1B: ddis tasks | 0 | 2 (tasks.go, discovery/tasks.go) | ~60 | 3 | 23 provisional |
| Phase 1C: Event wiring | 0 | 5 (parse, validate, drift, contradict, patch) | ~40 | 5 | APP-INV-020, 025 |
| Phase 2: 13 tests | 1 | 0 | ~500 | 13 | 13 → confirmed |
| Phase 3: LLM provider | 3 | 2 (go.mod, witness.go) | ~400 | 5 | 10 remaining |
| Phase 4: Tier 6 | 2 | 2 (consistency.go, contradict.go) | ~360 | 4 | APP-INV-051 new |
| TOTAL | 6 new | 13 modified | ~1,400 | 32 | 50/50 targeted |

EXPECTED OUTCOMES:
- Phase 1: ddis next says '23 provisional — upgrade evidence' instead of 'all aligned'
- Phase 2: 27→40 confirmed (80%)
- Phase 3: eval infrastructure ready, confidence calibrated
- Phase 4: 40→potentially 50 confirmed (100%), semantic contradictions detected
- Event wiring: 5 event types flowing to Stream 2 JSONL

payload.title: LLM-as-Judge Semantic Contradictions via Anthropic SDK

payload.content: Test architecture convergence: Progressive test levels (L1 synthetic, L2 integration) via Go build tags. Self-contained synthetic fixtures. Race detector compliant. Aligns test suite with APP-INV-002 (determinism), APP-INV-005 (self-containment), APP-INV-040 (progressive validation).

Gap analysis from external agent: 7 gaps identified in DDIS CLI authoring onramp. P0: Parser silently discards invariants when statement format doesn't match *italic*. P1: No manifest-to-module-stub generation (bootstrapping gap). P1: SQLite auto-creates empty DBs on wrong argument order. P1: APP-INV-048 VCS primacy has no mechanical validation check. P2: crystallize requires --module despite manifest bijection. P2: DB arg ordering inconsistent (search/query use second positional, others use first). P3: No bulk rename command.

FINDING: GAP G2 (P0 CRITICAL) — Parser Silent Invariant Discard. internal/parser/invariants.go line 59-62: when statement doesn't match *italic* regex (InvStatementRe), state resets to idle with zero diagnostic output. The function isInvariantComplete() at line 212-215 was written to catch this but is NEVER CALLED (dead code). This violates APP-INV-046 (Error Recovery Guidance). Any new spec author hitting format mismatch gets 0% coverage with no explanation. Affects: APP-INV-046, APP-INV-036.

FINDING: GAP G5a (P1) — SQLite Silent DB Creation. storage.Open() at internal/storage/db.go line 18-55 uses sql.Open('sqlite', dbPath) which auto-creates non-existent files via modernc.org/sqlite. When search/query arg order is wrong (db first, query second), the second positional arg becomes dbPath. A new empty DB is created with that name, schema applied, GetFirstSpecID finds no rows, and user sees 'no spec found' — completely masking the real error (wrong argument order). Live reproduction: ddis search manifest.ddis.db 'query' fails; ddis search 'query' manifest.ddis.db works.

FINDING: GAP G1 (P1) — Missing Manifest-to-Module-Stubs Morphism. The initialization category has: skeleton→(manifest+modules), parse→DB, init→workspace, crystallize→module-element. But no manifest→module-stubs arrow. When a user hand-authors manifest.yaml with modules declared but no .md files exist, ddis parse fails and there's no way to generate the stubs. The bilateral dual of ddis manifest sync (files→manifest) is missing: manifest→files. Formal: this is a fixed-point initialization problem in the lifecycle dependency graph.

FINDING: GAP G6 (P1) — APP-INV-048 VCS Primacy Unenforced. The invariant states 'forall ws in Workspaces: .ddis/events/stream-{1,2,3}.jsonl in VCS(ws) AND NOT in .gitignore(ws)'. This is well-specified but has NO mechanical validation check. ddis validate runs 17 checks but none verify VCS tracking of event streams. Recommendation: Add Check 18 that verifies .ddis/events/ exists, stream files not in .gitignore, and index.db IS in .gitignore (derived artifact).

FINDING: GAP G3 (P2) — Crystallize Module Auto-Detection. crystallize.go line 74 requires --module explicitly, but manifest modules.*.maintains creates a surjection from invariant IDs to module names. Given APP-INV-007, the manifest unambiguously maps it to query-validation. The JSON input already has owner and domain fields. Auto-detection is deterministic and unambiguous — about 30 LOC to implement.

FINDING: GAP G4 (P3) — No Bulk Rename Command. ddis patch single-occurrence enforcement is deliberate safety (uniqueness proof). But mechanical renames (INV-001→APP-INV-001) need high-arity replacement across all files. Recommendation: separate ddis rename command with --from/--to/--confirm, operating across all manifest module files, logging single oplog entry. Keeps patch surgical, adds rename for mechanical operations.

FINDING: GAP G5b (P2) — DB Argument Ordering Inconsistency. 22 of 33 commands use FindDB() with optional first positional arg. But search and query take DB as SECOND positional (query/target first). tx commands take DB as first but with subcommand-specific layouts. manifest sync uses hardcoded fallback paths. refine has triple-priority resolution (--spec flag → positional → FindDB). This inconsistency is a usability trap especially for search/query where wrong order causes the silent DB creation bug (G5a). Recommendation: normalize to --db flag across all 22 auto-discovery commands.

FAILURE MODE IDENTIFIED: Agent bypassed ddis discover workflow despite explicit instruction to 'Use the DDIS discovery workflow to start' and 'Use DDIS and ddis for everything.' Agent used ddis search/query/validate as investigation tools directly, treating them as the discovery mechanism. The correct workflow is: ddis discover FIRST (opens thread, records events to JSONL streams, establishes provenance chain), THEN use search/query/validate as supporting tools WITHIN that thread context. The distinction matters because discover records to the canonical temporal record (APP-INV-020, APP-INV-048), while search/query are stateless reads. This is the difference between 'doing research' and 'doing DDIS discovery' — the bilateral lifecycle requires the latter. Root cause: agent defaulted to familiar tool-use patterns instead of following the specified methodology. This must be treated as a hard constraint violation, not a soft preference.

We need a new CLI command 'ddis issue' that wraps 'gh issue create' to let users submit bugs/issues to the DDIS GitHub repo directly from the CLI. This is a thin alias/wrapper around gh CLI. Should support title, body, labels. Minimal spec footprint — one new ADR, possibly one invariant for the gh dependency graceful degradation.

FINDINGS: Next available IDs are APP-INV-057 and APP-ADR-044. Best module is workspace-ops (workspace artifact integration). Design: (1) APP-ADR-044: External Issue Tracker Integration via gh CLI — thin wrapper, not core dependency. (2) APP-INV-057: External Tool Graceful Degradation — ddis issue must fail with clear guidance when gh is not installed or not authenticated, never crash. Command surface: 'ddis issue [title]' with --body, --label flags. Shells out to 'gh issue create'. Recovery hint on missing gh binary. Guidance postscript after successful creation.

payload.title: External Issue Tracker Integration via gh CLI

payload.content: Beginning crystallization: 5 new invariants (APP-INV-057-061), 8 new ADRs (APP-ADR-044-051). Addresses 7 authoring onramp gaps + lifecycle reachability meta-invariant.
All 13 elements crystallized, frontmatter/constitution/manifest updated, 6 negative specs added. Beginning final verification.

Spec extension complete: 62/62 INVs (100%), 52/52 ADRs (100%), 55 negative specs, 39 glossary entries, 16/17 checks pass (Check 17 needs re-challenge), drift=1 (expected: new spec awaiting implementation). Ready for implementation phase.

Validation warning triage: 610 warnings across 7 checks have persisted unfixed. Investigating from first principles to classify each as bug, false positive, or improvement. Formal analysis of Check 3 (382 orphan sections), Check 4 (157 glossary), Check 5 (2 ownership), Check 11 (2 weight), Check 14 (1 stale witness), Check 16 (11 behavioral), Check 17 (55 challenge).
